actor_claims.actor_label: the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives
actor_claims.quote_text: the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives

event_claims.actor_label: the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives
event_claims.description: The merger agreement prohibits the Company, its affiliates, and their representatives from soliciting, initiating, encouraging, or facilitating alternative proposals or related inquiries, discussions, or offers.

event_claims.actor_label: the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives
event_claims.description: The merger agreement prohibits the Company, its affiliates, and their representatives from participating in discussions or negotiations or furnishing nonpublic information regarding an alternative proposal, except to notify the person of the non-solicitation provisions.
event_claims.quote_text: participate in any discussions or negotiations regarding an alternative proposal with, or furnish any nonpublic information regarding the Company or its subsidiaries in connection with an alternative proposal to, any person that has made, or is considering making an alternative proposal (except to notify such person as to the non-solicitation provisions of the merger agreement);

event_claims.actor_label: the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives
event_claims.description: The merger agreement prohibits the Company, its affiliates, and their representatives from entering into agreements relating to or providing for an alternative proposal, except permitted confidentiality agreements.
event_claims.quote_text: enter into any letter of intent, agreement in principle, merger or acquisition agreement or any other agreement relating to or providing for any alternative proposal (except for confidentiality agreements permitted under the merger agreement).

event_claims.description: Before the required stockholder vote, if the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party that the board judges to constitute or reasonably be expected to result in a superior proposal, the Company may provide nonpublic information after entering into an acceptable confidentiality agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: provide nonpublic information to the third party after entering into a confidentiality agreement acceptable under the terms of the merger agreement;

event_claims.description: Before the required stockholder vote, if the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party that the board judges to constitute or reasonably be expected to result in a superior proposal, the Company may engage in discussions or negotiations with the third party.
event_claims.quote_text: engage in discussions or negotiations with a third party with respect to the alternative proposal.

event_claims.description: To change its recommendation or terminate the merger agreement to accept a superior proposal, the Company must give Parent written notice of the board's intention and describe the superior proposal terms.
event_claims.quote_text: provide Parent written notice of the board’s intention to make a change in recommendation or to terminate the merger agreement with a description of the terms of the superior proposal;

event_claims.description: To change its recommendation or terminate the merger agreement to accept a superior proposal, the Company must tell Parent in writing its reasons for doing so.
event_claims.quote_text: provide to Parent in writing its reasons for making the change in recommendation or for terminating the merger agreement;

event_claims.description: Parent must be given three business days to propose changes to the merger agreement, with extensions for material changes in the superior proposal, and the Company must negotiate with Parent in good faith during that period.
event_claims.quote_text: provide Parent with three business days to propose changes to the terms of the merger agreement (which will be extended for additional two business day periods for any material change in the superior proposal), and during this period negotiate with Parent in good faith to make adjustments to the terms and conditions of the merger agreement.

event_claims.description: After Parent's match period and any extensions, the board must determine that the superior proposal remains superior to the merger proposal after considering Parent's proposed revisions.
event_claims.quote_text: Following the end of such three business day period (and any extension thereto), after taking into account any revisions to the terms and conditions of the merger agreement proposed by Parent, the board must determine that the superior proposal remains superior to the merger proposal.

event_claims.description: The Company may terminate the merger agreement before the Company stockholder meeting to accept a superior proposal if it pays Parent a $255 million termination fee and promptly enters into a definitive agreement for the superior proposal.
event_claims.quote_text:
at any time prior to the Company stockholder meeting to accept a “superior proposal” (defined below under The Merger Agreement — Other Covenants and Agreements — No Solicitation ) so
